Part B.key_evidence: The difference image shows a noisy, irregular residual with strong dipole/black-and-white pattern near the center, not a clean PSF. The science and reference panels show a bright star nearby (sgscore1=0.976, srmag1=12.87 at 3.9 arcsec), indicating the candidate sits adjacent to a very bright PS1 star. classtar=0.471 and fwhm=4.86 are not strongly point-like. ncovhist=2160 with only ndethist=2 means this position has been observed thousands of times with almost no detections, arguing against persistent variability at this exact spot.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: The residual pattern in the difference image is consistent with a subtraction artifact caused by PSF/astrometric mismatch from the very bright nearby star (r~12.9 at ~4 arcsec). The messy, non-PSF morphology and lack of repeated history despite massive coverage support a bogus/artifact classification.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An alternative is genuine stellar variability of the bright PS1 neighbor bleeding into the difference; however the candidate is offset from the star and the residual is not a clean PSF, making artifact more likely than a real variable star detection.
